compiled_state.title: onboarding: non-technical user prompted for technical config questions -> should apply reasonable defaults
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Incident Overview
/dso:onboarding asks non-technical users questions that require technical knowledge and should instead use reasonable defaults.

## Examples
- Which accessibility standard to target (e.g., WCAG 2.1 AA) — should default to WCAG 2.1 AA without asking
- Code linting/formatting tool preferences — should apply project-appropriate defaults (e.g., ruff for Python, eslint for JS)

## Expected vs Observed
- **Expected**: Non-technical users receive sensible defaults for purely technical setup questions; onboarding moves forward without asking for domain expertise the user doesn't have.
- **Observed**: /dso:onboarding presents technical questions (accessibility standards, linting tools) to all users regardless of technical level, creating friction and risk of misconfiguration.

## Affected Component
plugins/dso/skills/onboarding/SKILL.md — question sequencing and technical question policy

## Notes
Reported by user during interactive session. The fix should detect (or infer from prior answers) whether the user is technical, and skip or default-fill technical questions accordingly.
